findings.title: BUK963R3-60E power MOSFETs classified as transformers
findings.description: T101, T402, and T403 are BUK963R3-60E (NXP 60V N-channel power MOSFET, TO-263-2 package) used as high-current heater drivers for the print bed, extruder 1, and fan 1. The analyzer classifies all three as type 'transformer' because the custom library symbol 'FHNW-Pro4E-FS19T8-Library:BUK963R3-60E' has all three pins typed as 'passive' and its name does not match the standard transistor keyword patterns. As a result the component_types count shows 3 transformers and 5 transistors instead of 8 transistors (or 5 small-signal + 3 power MOSFETs). None of T101/T402/T403 appear in the transistor_circuits section of signal_analysis.
findings.source_evidence: Component 'type': 'transformer' at JSON lines 14288, 14381, 2866; BOM entry references [T101, T402, T403] with type 'transformer' at line 1495; source lib_id 'FHNW-Pro4E-FS19T8-Library:BUK963R3-60E'

findings.title: Fan MOSFETs drain/source nets reported in inverted order
findings.description: T401, T404, T405 (PMV40UN2 N-channel MOSFETs) are low-side fan switches with source connected to GND and drain connected to the fan load (Fan1_pwr, Fan2_pwr, Fan3_pwr). The analyzer reports drain_net=GND and source_net=Fan1_pwr/Fan2_pwr/Fan3_pwr — the reverse of the actual topology. Inspection of the schematic at appendix_SENSORS_POWER.sch confirms: GND symbol is at y=1750-1850 connecting to the source pin of T401 (2N7002 symbol pin 2), while Fan1_pwr label is at y=1350 connecting to the drain pin (pin 3). The drain_is_power flag is also set to true (GND is not a power supply), while source_is_ground is false instead of true.
findings.source_evidence: transistor_circuits entries for T401/T404/T405 in signal_analysis show drain_net='GND', source_net='Fan1_pwr'/'Fan2_pwr'/'Fan3_pwr'; schematic coordinates confirm pin 3 (drain) at y=1350 connects to Fan1_pwr label, pin 2 (source) at y=1750 connects to GND

findings.title: Fan MOSFETs incorrectly identified as LED drivers with wrong load components
findings.description: All three fan MOSFET circuits (T401, T404, T405) are reported with load_type='led' and a led_driver record pointing to D401 (LED_YEL) with current resistor R422 (100k). This is incorrect on two counts: (1) the actual load is a 24V DC fan connected via a JST connector on the drain side, not an LED; (2) D401 is located on the E1 extruder MOSFET gate circuit (as a status indicator LED driven from the E1_SIG_ON net), not connected to the fan MOSFETs. The same spurious led_driver record (D401/R422) is repeated identically for all three fan MOSFETs. The 100k resistor value for an LED further confirms the misidentification — a proper LED driver uses a current-limiting resistor of typically 100-470 ohms, not 100k.
findings.source_evidence: transistor_circuits entries for T401/T404/T405 each have led_driver with led_ref='D401', led_value='LED_YEL', current_resistor='R422', resistor_ohms=100000.0; D401 is at position (4450,5750) in appendix_SENSORS_POWER.sch in the E1 heater section, not connected to fan MOSFETs

findings.title: TMC2660 stepper current-sense networks classified as voltage dividers
findings.description: The 8 pairs of 10R/0R15 resistors (R607/R608, R603/R604, R605/R606, R601/R602, R615/R616, R611/R612, R613/R614, R609/R610) are stepper motor current-sense networks for the TMC2660 drivers. Each pair consists of: a 10R termination resistor connected between a TMC2660 current-monitor output pin and a measurement midpoint, and a 0R15 shunt resistor from the midpoint to GND, with the AIN analog input pin of the TMC2660 reading the voltage across the shunt. The analyzer classifies these as 8 voltage dividers with ratio 0.014778 (0.15/10.15). While the topology is topologically a divider, the functional purpose is motor current sensing via a shunt resistor. The mid_point_connections correctly identify some as connected to TMC2660 OA1 (op-amp output) pins, which confirms the current-sense function.
findings.source_evidence: voltage_dividers section lists 8 entries with r_top values 10R and r_bottom values 0R15, ratio=0.014778; mid_point_connections show U602/U601/U604/U603 pin 7 (OA1); schematic appendix_TMC2660_X.sch shows these are ISENSE networks for TMC2660 stepper drivers

findings.title: SD card detect RC filter input_net reported as SD_DAT0 instead of +3.3V
findings.description: The RC filter formed by R216 (10k) and C203 (100n) is reported as input_net='SD_DAT0', output_net='SD_DETECT'. In the actual schematic (appendix_STM32F103.sch), R216 is a pullup resistor with one end tied to +3.3V and the other end at the SD_DETECT net, with C203 to GND forming a debounce filter. SD_DAT0 shares the same +3.3V pullup bus as other SD card lines but does not directly drive the SD_DETECT RC network. The input_net should be '+3.3V', not 'SD_DAT0'. This occurs because SD_DAT0 and the +3.3V pullup rails share connectivity at the same y-coordinate region and the analyzer selected the wrong net as the filter input.
findings.source_evidence: rc_filters entry for R216/C203 shows input_net='SD_DAT0', output_net='SD_DETECT'; appendix_STM32F103.sch at line 2526 shows +3.3V power symbol at (13350,2150) connecting directly to R216 pin 1, with SD_DETECT at the output

findings.title: STM32 NRST pin connected to FAN_EXT net without RC filter
findings.description: The design_observations correctly reports that U201 (STM32F103ZETx) NRST pin (pin 25) connects to net 'FAN_EXT' with no pullup and no filter capacitor. This is a legitimate schematic fact: the net is labeled FAN_EXT but carries the NRST signal to connector J203 pin 12 (the external JTAG/debug connector). The missing RC filter on NRST is a real design concern — STM32 datasheet recommends a 100nF filter capacitor on NRST. The 'FAN_EXT' net name is misleading and likely a schematic naming error by the designer, not an analyzer error. This observation is accurate.
findings.source_evidence: design_observations entry category='reset_pin', component='U201', pin='NRST', net='FAN_EXT', has_pullup=false, has_filter_cap=false; net FAN_EXT shows connections to J203 pin 12 and U201 pin 25 (NRST) in the nets data

findings.title: LM2675M regulators missing input_rail identification
findings.description: Both switching regulators U101 (LM2675M-3.3, generates +3.3V) and U102 (LM2675M-5, generates +5V) show input_rail=null in the power_regulators section. In the schematic, both regulators are fed from +24V (the main board supply). The LM2675M VIN pin connects to +24V through an input filter inductor (L101 for U101, L102 for U102), but the analyzer cannot trace through the LC filter to identify the source rail as +24V. The topology is also reported as 'unknown' rather than 'buck' — the LM2675M is a simple buck (step-down) converter. These missing fields reduce the usefulness of the power topology summary.
findings.source_evidence: power_regulators entries for U101 and U102 both have input_rail=null and topology='unknown'; lc_filters section correctly shows L101/C103 and L102/C109 as the input filters; source schematic shows +24V feeding L101/L102 inputs

findings.summary: 3D printer controller (STM32F103+ESP32, KiCad 5, 264 components). BUK963R3-60E power MOSFETs (T101/T402/T403) misclassified as transformers due to passive pin types. Fan MOSFET drain/source nets inverted. Fan MOSFETs falsely linked to LED D401 as LED drivers. LM2675M buck regulators have null input_rail and unknown topology.

correct.description: Thermistor RC filters (R401/C401 etc, 3.39Hz ADC anti-alias) correctly detected
ESP32 auto-reset BJT pair (Q1/Q2 2N3904) correctly identified
4 protection devices (fuses F101/F401/F402, USBLC6 USB ESD) correct
LC input filters for buck regulators (47uH/10nF, 232kHz) correct

incorrect.description: T101/T402/T403 BUK963R3-60E power MOSFETs (60V N-ch, heater switches) misclassified as type='transformer' — custom library symbol has all pins typed 'passive', name doesn't match transistor patterns. Missing from transistor_circuits entirely.
T401/T404/T405 PMV40UN2 fan MOSFETs have drain/source inverted: drain_net='GND', source_net='Fan1_pwr' — should be source=GND, drain=Fan_pwr (load)
T401/T404/T405 each get false led_driver record pointing to D401 LED_YEL with R422 (100k) — D401 is in a different circuit section, no connectivity to fan MOSFETs
LM2675M-3.3 (U101) and LM2675M-5 (U102) both show input_rail=null and topology='unknown' — can't trace through input inductor to find +24V source
SD_DETECT RC filter R216/C203 has wrong input_net='SD_DAT0' — should be +3.3V (R216 pulls up from +3.3V)

suggestions: Component classification should check for MOSFET-like footprints (TO-263, DPAK) even when pin types are passive
Verify drain/source assignment using pin position relative to gate
LED driver detection requires net connectivity
Buck regulator: trace through input LC filter to find source rail

accurate_detections: Crystal circuit: SG-210STF active oscillator correctly identified as active_oscillator with output to RCC_OSC_IN, no load caps (accurate)
Thermistor RC filters: R401/C401, R405/C403, R415/C407 correctly identified as low-pass filters for thermistor ADC inputs (ADC_TEMP_BED, ADC_TEMP_E1, ADC_TEMP_PCB) with 3.39 Hz cutoff
Endstop debounce filters: R402/C402, R406/C404, R410/C406, R409/C405 (10k/10u, 1.59 Hz) correctly identified for limit switch inputs (SW_End_X, SW_End_Y, SW_End_Z, SW_End_Filament_1)
Button debounce: R233/C215 and R234/C216 (10k/100n, 159 Hz) for BTN_USER_1 and BTN_USER_2 correctly identified
USB line series resistors: R504/C1 (27R/33n, 178 kHz) correctly identified as USB differential pair termination/filter
LM2675M regulators detected with correct fixed output voltages (3.3V and 5V via fixed_suffix method)
Protection devices: F101 (20A main fuse), F401 (3A), F402 (10A), U1 (USBLC6-2SC6 USB ESD) all correctly identified
ESP32 auto-reset BJTs: Q1/Q2 (2N3904) correctly identified as the ESP32 RTS/DTR auto-reset circuit with collector=ESP_DTR/ESP_RTS and emitter=ESP32_GPIO0/ESP32_RESET
VBUS voltage divider: R503 (4k7) / R506 (10k) from VBUS to GND correctly identified as USB presence detection divider (ratio 0.68)
LC input filters: L101/C103 and L102/C109 (47uH/10nF) correctly identified as buck converter input EMI filters at 232 kHz resonance
Decoupling analysis: All 6 power rails (+3.3V, +24V, +3.3VP, +3.3VA, +5V, VBUS) correctly analyzed with bulk/bypass coverage
24V bulk capacitors: 4x 470uF (C633-C636) on +24V rail correctly identified as motor/heater bulk supply capacitors
